title: Midnight-Craving Korean Paneer Jjigae
description: A spicy, savory, and addictive Korean-inspired dish featuring paneer, crispy garlic, and a hint of ginger.
instructions: Mortar and pestle pound 3 cloves of garlic and 1-inch piece of ginger until you get a smooth paste.
Heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic-ginger paste and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Add 8 oz of paneer and cook for 5 minutes, until the paneer is coated in the sauce.
In a small bowl, mix 2 tbsp of cornstarch with 2 tbsp of water until smooth.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the pan and cook for 1 minute, until the sauce thickens.
Add 2 tsp of Korean chili flakes, 1 tsp of fish sauce, 1 tsp of soy sauce, 1 tsp of brown sugar, and 1 tsp of rice vinegar. Cook for 1 minute, until the flavors are combined.
Transfer the pan to the oven and bake at 400°F for 10 minutes, until the top is crispy.
Garnish with 1/4 cup of green onions and serve immediately.
